title: Heterophragma quadriloculare
taxonomy: Heterophragma quadriloculare is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. This family is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as the bignonias. Bignoniaceae is a member of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further divided into several suborders and includes many families of flowering plants. Members of the Bignoniaceae family are often known for their showy flowers and climbing habit.
intro: Heterophragma quadriloculare, also known as the four-loculed heterophragma, is a plant species in the family Bignoniaceae, commonly found in tropical regions, characterized by its unique, four-loculed fruit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Heterophragma quadriloculare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal decoctions, teas, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into various folk remedies, such as cough syrups and respiratory tonics.
